Figure 2:Three empirical motivations for Spectral Forcing.(a)Radial 2D-DCT power spectra of the three toy distributions, overlaid on ImageNet-256 (insets: samples).(b)Converged 1D toy denoiser: per-bandlog10⁡(MSEnet/MSEzero)\log_{10}(\mathrm{MSE}_{\mathrm{net}}/\mathrm{MSE}_{\mathrm{zero}})on the(t,k)(t,k)plane reveals three regions:signal recovery(low-kkwedge, the only region of true data-distribution work),closed-form denoising(lowtt, highkk),predict-zero(hightt, highkk).(c)2D toy DiT: input-side time-conditional low-pass vs. patch size (h=64h{=}64,α=2\alpha{=}2). Helps at coarsepp; starves at very finepp.(d)Same operator across data spectra atp=8p{=}8. Helps on power-law (analytical≫\gglinear), neutral on structured, hurts on rectangles where high-frequency content is essential signal.

Figure 3:The wedge transfers from the toy to real ImageNet.Per-bandlog10⁡(MSEnet/MSEzero​-​pred.)\log_{10}(\mathrm{MSE}_{\mathrm{net}}/\mathrm{MSE}_{\mathrm{zero\text{-}pred.}})for a trained JiT-700M/32 baseline (6060ep, EMA weights). The three regions identified inFig.˜2(b) are visible: closed-form denoising (smalltt, highkk), the signal-recovery wedge (lowkk, growing withtt), and a predict-zero region (largett, mid–highkk) where the network is no better than a zero predictor.

Figure 4:Multi-epoch behaviour of Spectral Forcing on ImageNet-256.(a) FID-50k trajectories (log-scale) for JiT-130M/32 and JiT-700M/32; solid: baseline, dashed: Linear-SF; the headline 60-epoch gap at JiT-700M/32 is annotated. (b) FID improvement ofSFover the matched-epoch baseline. JiT-130M/32 (blue) compresses to within evaluator noise by 100 ep then holds a small persistent margin at 200 ep (+1.5%+1.5\%); JiT-700M/32 (red) retains an asymptotic component out to 120 ep (+8.0%+8.0\%); JiT-130M/16 (gray) is regime-bounded.

Figure 5:Spectral Forcing transfers to native vision-language models: DPG-Bench overall and per-category.SenseNova-U1[8]at stage-1 100k steps; identical baseline (BL) andSFrecipe except for the input operator. Top bar is the overall headline; categories below are sorted bySF−BL\textsc{SF}{}-\mathrm{BL}. SF bars are coloured by win/loss against BL;SFwins99of1313subcategories.

Figure 6:Qualitative samples on ImageNet-256.JiT-700M/32 at120120epochs, baseline (B, top row of each block) vs. Linear-SF(SF, bottom row), three sample indices per class, same class label and same sample index per column.
